topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the application form, the crisp white paper blurring slightly under his gaze. Across the desk, Maya was humming softly, tapping away at her own submission. Every time her hand moved, a strange warmth bloomed in Liam’s chest, tightening his throat. He reread the job description—Marketing Coordinator. It was a perfect fit, a chance to be near her, to share the same workspace. A sudden, sharp intake of breath made him acutely aware of the way the light caught the curve of her neck as she leaned over her keyboard. *Just send mine in,* he thought, a reckless impulse seizing him. He snatched the pen, his hand trembling just enough to make the ink smudge slightly on the corner of the sheet.

[story 2]

"You're going for the same role, aren't you?" Chloe murmured, her voice laced with a knowing amusement as she watched Alex meticulously proofread his cover letter. Alex felt a flush creep up his neck, a distinct heat radiating outward. He tried to focus on the formatting, but his eyes kept drifting to Chloe’s profile—the way her hair fell across her cheek, the subtle tilt of her head when she laughed at something on her screen. He felt a desperate need to impress her, to be the one who captured her attention. When he finally finished, he pushed the envelope across the desk with unnecessary force. "Good luck," he managed, his voice sounding unexpectedly rough.

[story 3]

Sarah watched David across the coffee station. He was animated, gesturing with his hands as he discussed his qualifications, and the sound of his voice—low, confident, utterly engaging—made the air around her feel thin. She fiddled with the strap of her messenger bag, suddenly feeling clumsy and overly conscious of her own presence. This opportunity felt monumental; it was the gateway to getting closer to him, to seeing him in a different light. Her palms grew damp against the cool metal of the mug she held. When it was time to submit, Sarah’s fingers moved slowly, deliberately, as if each keystroke was a delicate maneuver. She glanced up, catching his eye for a fleeting second, and a small, hopeful smile touched her lips before she quickly looked back down at the screen.
